# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Use safe data-only formats and validate the resulting structure/types before use.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Use safe data-only formats such as JSON and validate structure/types before use.
- Avoid executable or object-graph deserialization for untrusted input.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not deserialize untrusted data with unsafe native object deserializers.
- Do not execute or instantiate attacker-controlled object graphs.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

# lib/secure_json_session_store.rb
require 'json'
require 'base64'
require 'openssl'

class SecureJsonSessionStore < ActionDispatch::Session::AbstractStore
  SESSION_DATA_MAX_SIZE = 4096 # Cookie size limit
  ALLOWED_VALUE_TYPES = [String, Integer, Float, TrueClass, FalseClass, NilClass, Array, Hash].freeze

  def initialize(app, options = {})
    super
    @secret = options[:secret] || Rails.application.secret_key_base
    raise ArgumentError, "A secret key must be configured for session signing" if @secret.blank?
  end

  private

  def find_session(req, session_id)
    session_id ||= generate_sid
    cookie_value = req.cookie_jar[:session]

    if cookie_value.present?
      data = deserialize_and_verify(cookie_value)
      if data.is_a?(Hash) && validate_session_structure(data)
        return [session_id, data]
      end
    end

    [session_id, {}]
  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.warn("SecureJsonSessionStore: Failed to load session: #{e.message}")
    [generate_sid, {}]
  end

  def write_session(req, session_id, session_data, _options)
    unless session_data.is_a?(Hash) && validate_session_structure(session_data)
      Rails.logger.warn("SecureJsonSessionStore: Invalid session data structure rejected")
      return false
    end

    serialized = sign_and_serialize(session_data)

    if serialized.bytesize > SESSION_DATA_MAX_SIZE
      Rails.logger.warn("SecureJsonSessionStore: Session data exceeds maximum cookie size")
      return false
    end

    req.cookie_jar[:session] = {
      value: serialized,
      httponly: true,
      secure: Rails.env.production?,
      same_site: :lax
    }

    session_id
  end

  def delete_session(req, session_id, _options)
    req.cookie_jar.delete(:session)
    generate_sid
  end

  # --- Serialization using JSON (safe, data-only format) ---

  def sign_and_serialize(data)
    json_payload = JSON.generate(sanitize_data(data))
    encoded_payload = Base64.strict_encode64(json_payload)
    signature = compute_hmac(encoded_payload)
    "#{encoded_payload}--#{signature}"
  end

  def deserialize_and_verify(signed_cookie)
    parts = signed_cookie.split("--", 2)
    return nil unless parts.length == 2

    encoded_payload, signature = parts

    unless secure_compare(compute_hmac(encoded_payload), signature)
      Rails.logger.warn("SecureJsonSessionStore: HMAC signature verification failed - possible tampering")
      return nil
    end

    json_payload = Base64.strict_decode64(encoded_payload)
    parsed = JSON.parse(json_payload)

    unless parsed.is_a?(Hash)
      Rails.logger.warn("SecureJsonSessionStore: Deserialized data is not a Hash")
      return nil
    end

    parsed
  rescue JSON::ParserError, ArgumentError => e
    Rails.logger.warn("SecureJsonSessionStore: Deserialization error: #{e.message}")
    nil
  end

  # --- Validation ---

  def validate_session_structure(data)
    return false unless data.is_a?(Hash)

    data.all? do |key, value|
      key.is_a?(String) && validate_value(value)
    end
  end

  def validate_value(value)
    case value
    when String, Integer, Float, TrueClass, FalseClass, NilClass
      true
    when Array
      value.all? { |v| validate_value(v) }
    when Hash
      value.all? { |k, v| k.is_a?(String) && validate_value(v) }
    else
      false
    end
  end

  def sanitize_data(data)
    return {} unless data.is_a?(Hash)

    data.each_with_object({}) do |(key, value), sanitized|
      str_key = key.to_s
      sanitized[str_key] = sanitize_value(value)
    end
  end

  def sanitize_value(value)
    case value
    when String, Integer, Float, TrueClass, FalseClass, NilClass
      value
    when Symbol
      value.to_s
    when Array
      value.map { |v| sanitize_value(v) }
    when Hash
      value.each_with_object({}) { |(k, v), h| h[k.to_s] = sanitize_value(v) }
    else
      value.to_s
    end
  end

  # --- HMAC ---

  def compute_hmac(payload)
    OpenSSL::HMAC.hexdigest("SHA256", @secret, payload)
  end

  def secure_compare(a, b)
    return false unless a.bytesize == b.bytesize
    ActiveSupport::SecurityUtils.secure_compare(a, b)
  end
end
